name: Los Angeles Lee
description_short: Los Angeles Lee Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit credit union established in 1964, serving over 76 members with savings accounts, auto loans, and personal banking services.
description_long:
Los Angeles Lee Federal Credit Union was established in 1964 and is headquartered in Los Angeles, California. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution serving specialized professional associations and trade organizations in the Los Angeles area.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial products including savings accounts, share draft accounts, money market accounts, share certificates (CDs), auto loans, personal loans, and mortgage loans. Members can access online banking, mobile app services for iOS and Android, online bill pay, financial counseling, and financial calculators. The institution emphasizes competitive rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks due to its not-for-profit structure.

Los Angeles Lee distinguishes itself by serving professional associations and trade organizations rather than the general public, focusing on economic growth within specialized sectors. The credit union maintains a single branch location at 964 North Hill Street in Los Angeles and has stewardship of $639,700 in assets. The institution provides access to routing number 322078516 and actively maintains online and mobile banking platforms for member convenience.

However, the credit union operates with significant limitations. With only 76 members and zero ATMs, the institution has minimal infrastructure and accessibility. The lobby is currently closed, and the organization lists zero dedicated employees as of 2026, suggesting operational constraints. The very small membership base and single branch location make this a highly specialized institution suitable only for eligible members within specific professional associations or trade organizations. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Auto loans for cars, trucks, and SUVs
pros: Not-for-profit structure offers competitive rates and lower fees than for-profit banks
Established institution with 60+ years of history (founded 1964)
Multiple account types available including savings, money market, CDs, and share drafts
Offers auto loans, personal loans, and mortgage loans
Online banking, mobile app, and online bill pay services available for members
Provides financial counseling and educational tools like calculators
Used vehicle auto loan rate of 2.99% is competitive
Member-owned cooperative structure aligns institutional incentives with member interests
cons: Extremely limited membership (only 76 members) restricts growth and service diversity
No ATM network (0 ATMs) requires members to rely solely on branch banking
Single branch location limits physical accessibility
Lobby currently closed, creating access barriers for in-person services
Zero dedicated employees listed as of 2026 suggests severe operational constraints or data anomalies
Restricted membership eligibility limited to professional associations and trade organizations only
Very small asset base ($639,700) limits lending capacity and product sophistication
best_for: Members of eligible professional associations or trade organizations in Los Angeles
Individuals seeking not-for-profit financial institution values and lower fee structures
Auto and personal loan borrowers in specialized professional sectors
Members prioritizing long-standing institutional stability and mission-driven banking
diagnosis: Los Angeles Lee FCU is best suited for members of specific professional associations and trade organizations who meet membership eligibility requirements and value not-for-profit banking with competitive rates. The critical caveat is that this institution operates with severe infrastructure limitations—zero ATMs, a single closed branch, only 76 members, and unclear staffing—making it inaccessible to the general public and potentially unreliable for regular banking needs.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
